title: Resting-state EEG data before and after cognitive activity across the adult lifespan and a 5-year follow-up
description: This dataset comprises 64-channel resting-state EEG recordings from 608 healthy adults aged 20-70 years, collected as part of the Dortmund Vital Study. Recordings were obtained before and after a 2-hour cognitive task battery, with measurements at baseline (2016-2023) and approximately 5-year follow-up (starting 2021) for 208 participants. The study investigates spontaneous brain electrical activity across the adult lifespan to elucidate normal aging processes and differences in brain activity relevant to cognitive health.
methods_description: Resting-state EEG was recorded using a 64-channel elastic cap (actiCap, Brain Products GmbH) arranged according to the 10-20 system with FCz as online reference. Recordings were acquired with a BrainVision Brainamp DC amplifier at 1000 Hz sampling rate with online 250 Hz low-pass filtering. Each session consisted of three-minute recordings with eyes closed followed by eyes open, both before and after a 2-hour cognitive task block. Impedances were maintained below 10 kOhm.

funding_references.funder_name: The Dortmund Vital Study is funded by the institute’s budget (no grant number). Thus, the study design, collection, management, analysis, interpretation of data, writing of the report, and the decision to submit the report for publication is not influenced or biased by any sponsor.
